description: Bracket-WRITE on a map is forbidden (§59.7). Read is bracket (@m[k]); write is method (.insert). A @m["DAL"] = 4500 bracket-write on a map-typed cell fires E-MAP-BRACKET-WRITE (the §34 error partition), with a fix-it naming .insert(k,v). The cycles-prereq routes bracket-writes through the COW array/object path, which would corrupt a map's internal representation. This case uses the function-body form; the inline event-handler form (onclick=${@m[k]=v}) — previously an inline-handler diagnostic-coverage gap — is now covered by the sibling case bracket-write-inline-handler-pos.
rationale: §59.7 @m[k] = v bracket-write on a map-typed cell (at any nesting level) is E-MAP-BRACKET-WRITE; the type-system gates a bracket-write whose receiver is map-typed before the COW lowering.
